items.title: Trump calls Walmart ‘patriotic’ for cutting beef prices — in a stark change of tune
items.description: The president hasn’t always approved of Walmart’s moves, having criticized the chain’s response to tariffs last year.

items.title: The Nasdaq-100 has been far more volatile than the S&P 500. Now add SpaceX to the mix.
items.description: While SpaceX is due to join the Nasdaq-100 on Tuesday, it won’t become part of the S&P 500 for at least another year — likely furthering the volatility spread between those two indexes.

items.title: U.S.-Belgium match could draw record TV audience amid the Trump World Cup controversy
items.description: Some industry experts are predicting all-time viewing records for a U.S. soccer game

items.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity
items.description: Your share of the AI wealth is a right — not a handout. Here is how we claw back our money.

items.title: Your investing expectations are more than double the reality. Here is the tough truth.
items.description: Long-term real returns above 10% annualized are exceedingly rare.

items.title: Stocks rally when Congress goes on summer break. Here’s why.
items.description: Stock prices face greater volatility when lawmakers are active, driven entirely by the regulatory uncertainty they create.

items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. Your retirement plan might disagree.
items.description: More investors want their money to reflect their values. It feels righteous — but the reality is a lot messier.

items.title: If the stock market’s double bubble bursts, it could usher in the next crash
items.description: While valuations still look extreme relative to history, the recent pace of corporate earnings growth has also meaningfully diverged from the long-term trend.

items.title: Micron’s stock gains, signaling a ‘return to optimism’ about the chip sector
items.description: Investors are looking forward to Samsung’s earnings and SK Hynix’s ADR listing, experts say.
